metadata.title:
VI. W.P. HAS DELAYED


BRIDGE WORK TILL FALL

pages.raw_text:
The bridge gang that is to con-
struct the piers for the north chan
mel bridge at Howard st. has COM
pleted the building of heavy
barge which will be used to carry
the cables and sounding apparatus.
1t is stated that frost will be on the
ground before the piers are COM
pleted. It begins to look now as
II the actual construction of the
new concrete bridges at Howard st.
will be delayed until fall on ac-
count Of the dilatory tactics of the
city council evidently following out
the dictatiOn Of the Washington
Water Power Co.


This company has entered objec-
ton after objection, seemingly for
NO other reason than to delay the
work. First 1t must have its own
engineer inspect the plans. This
engineer could not report until the
return from somewhere of d. l..
Huntington; then the bridge wasn't
planned just right and new plans
were asked for. Then the COM
pany wants another imported and
expensive engineer to revise the
plans and act as consulting en-
gineer. And the council stood for
all that.


Here the matter is tied up. The
Washington Water Power Co., per-
haps, in the far distant future, will
report to the council that its en-
gineer is to be had from somewhere
or other. Until 1t does, 1t is doubt
ed II the actual bridge construction
will be commenced.
